text.title: multi-agent-collaborative Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for multi-agent-collaborative. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The synthesis step MUST receive all worker outputs simultaneously in a single call, not iteratively. Never pass one agent's contribution first and then layer others on top.
rule.rationale: Iterative merging causes anchoring bias: the first contribution shapes all subsequent synthesis regardless of its quality. Simultaneous injection gives each contribution equal weight.

rule.statement: Set a hard cap on max_iterations (default 5). Add a per-run token budget check after each iteration; abort the collaboration if the budget is exceeded and proceed to synthesis with what has been collected.
rule.rationale: Without a budget ceiling, N agents × M iterations can exhaust tokens silently. Collaborative patterns are the most expensive multi-agent shape — budget enforcement is non-optional.
